result.stdout:
Description:
  Plan and execute deployment to the remote server based on changes made in git since the last release (tag)

Usage:
  kickflip [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  deploy <path>  Kick your git changes and flip them over to that remote server! [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-kickflip-2.0.10-61f5da2d3578452ebdc697359f382504]
  github         Integration with github to allow kickflip to work in your existing Github workflow.

commands.payload:
Description:
  Plan and execute deployment to the remote server based on changes made in git since the last release (tag)

Usage:
  kickflip [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  deploy <path>  Kick your git changes and flip them over to that remote server! [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-kickflip-2.0.10-61f5da2d3578452ebdc697359f382504]
  github         Integration with github to allow kickflip to work in your existing Github workflow.

result.stdout:
Description:
  Kick your git changes and flip them over to that remote server!

Usage:
  kickflip deploy [<path>] [options]

Arguments:
  <path>  Path to the folder containing the git repository for Git based deployment modes or the folder to use in folder deployment. The path is an absolute path. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-kickflip-2.0.10-61f5da2d3578452ebdc697359f382504]

Options:
  --mode <Folder|GitHubMergePR|Tags>   The find mode to use when trying to determine the starting point to compare changes too HEAD with. [default: Tags]
  --deployment-path <deployment-path>  Deployment path on the remote server to deploy to. The deployment path is relative to the root of the user account on the remote server. [default: /]
  --hostname <hostname> (REQUIRED)     Hostname of the remote server
  --port <port>                        Port of the remote server [default: 22]
  --username <username> (REQUIRED)     Authentication username for the remote server
  --password <password> (REQUIRED)     Authentication password for the remote server
  --dry                                Does a dry run of the deployment to see what would be done. No changes will be made to the remote server. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Kick your git changes and flip them over to that remote server!

Usage:
  kickflip deploy [<path>] [options]

Arguments:
  <path>  Path to the folder containing the git repository for Git based deployment modes or the folder to use in folder deployment. The path is an absolute path. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-kickflip-2.0.10-61f5da2d3578452ebdc697359f382504]

Options:
  --mode <Folder|GitHubMergePR|Tags>   The find mode to use when trying to determine the starting point to compare changes too HEAD with. [default: Tags]
  --deployment-path <deployment-path>  Deployment path on the remote server to deploy to. The deployment path is relative to the root of the user account on the remote server. [default: /]
  --hostname <hostname> (REQUIRED)     Hostname of the remote server
  --port <port>                        Port of the remote server [default: 22]
  --username <username> (REQUIRED)     Authentication username for the remote server
  --password <password> (REQUIRED)     Authentication password for the remote server
  --dry                                Does a dry run of the deployment to see what would be done. No changes will be made to the remote server. [default: False]
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Integration with github to allow kickflip to work in your existing Github workflow.

Usage:
  kickflip github [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  pr, pull-request <local-path>  Adds a comment to the pull request with the changes that will be deployed to the remote server. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-kickflip-2.0.10-61f5da2d3578452ebdc697359f382504]

commands.payload:
Description:
  Integration with github to allow kickflip to work in your existing Github workflow.

Usage:
  kickflip github [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  pr, pull-request <local-path>  Adds a comment to the pull request with the changes that will be deployed to the remote server. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-kickflip-2.0.10-61f5da2d3578452ebdc697359f382504]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Adds a comment to the pull request with the changes that will be deployed to the remote server.

Usage:
  kickflip github pull-request [<local-path>] [options]

Arguments:
  <local-path>  Local path to the folder containing the git repository. The local path is an absolute path. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-kickflip-2.0.10-61f5da2d3578452ebdc697359f382504]

Options:
  --mode <Folder|GitHubMergePR|Tags>   The find mode to use when trying to determine the starting point to compare changes too HEAD with. [default: Tags]
  --deployment-path <deployment-path>  Deployment path on the remote server to deploy to. The deployment path is relative to the root of the user account on the remote server. [default: /]
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)             The repository where the PR resides. Should be in the format of <owner>/<repository>.
  --ref <ref> (REQUIRED)               The fully-formed ref of the branch or tag that triggered the workflow run. The ref given is fully-formed for pull requests it is refs/pull/<pr_number>/merge. See the GITHUB_REF variable in the GitHub Actions documentation. https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/environment-variables
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)           The Github token or Personal access token to use for authentication. See the GITHUB_TOKEN variable in the GitHub Actions documentation. https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/automatic-token-authentication
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Adds a comment to the pull request with the changes that will be deployed to the remote server.

Usage:
  kickflip github pull-request [<local-path>] [options]

Arguments:
  <local-path>  Local path to the folder containing the git repository. The local path is an absolute path. [default: /tmp/inspectra-static-kickflip-2.0.10-61f5da2d3578452ebdc697359f382504]

Options:
  --mode <Folder|GitHubMergePR|Tags>   The find mode to use when trying to determine the starting point to compare changes too HEAD with. [default: Tags]
  --deployment-path <deployment-path>  Deployment path on the remote server to deploy to. The deployment path is relative to the root of the user account on the remote server. [default: /]
  --repo <repo> (REQUIRED)             The repository where the PR resides. Should be in the format of <owner>/<repository>.
  --ref <ref> (REQUIRED)               The fully-formed ref of the branch or tag that triggered the workflow run. The ref given is fully-formed for pull requests it is refs/pull/<pr_number>/merge. See the GITHUB_REF variable in the GitHub Actions documentation. https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/environment-variables
  --token <token> (REQUIRED)           The Github token or Personal access token to use for authentication. See the GITHUB_TOKEN variable in the GitHub Actions documentation. https://docs.github.com/en/actions/security-guides/automatic-token-authentication
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information
